.

obj.content: Kieran thinks the packing advice given by Jane's grandfather is
obj.html_content: Kieran thinks the packing advice given by Jane's grandfather is
common sense.
hard to follow.
over-protective.

obj.content: How does Jane feel about the books her grandfather has given her
obj.html_content: How does Jane feel about the books her grandfather has given her
They are not worth keeping.

obj.content: They should go to a collector.
obj.html_content: They should go to a collector.

obj.content: They have sentimental value for her.
obj.html_content: They have sentimental value for her.

obj.html_content: Jane and Kieran agree that hardback books should be
put out on display.
given as gifts to visitors.
more attractively designed.

obj.html_content: While talking about taking a book from a shelf, Jane

obj.content: describes the mistakes other people make doing it.
obj.html_content: describes the mistakes other people make doing it.

obj.content: reflects on a significant childhood experience.
obj.html_content: reflects on a significant childhood experience.

obj.html_content: explains why some books are easier to remove than others.

obj.content: What do Jane and Kieran suggest about new books?
obj.html_content: What do Jane and Kieran suggest about new books?

obj.content: Their parents liked buying them as presents.
obj.html_content: Their parents liked buying them as presents.

obj.content: They would like to buy more of them.
obj.html_content: They would like to buy more of them.
Not everyone can afford them.

sentence.content: Kieran: So Jane – you’ll be off to Denmark soon to do your work placement.
sentence.content_zh: 基兰：所以简-你很快就要去丹麦实习了。

sentence.content: Jane: Yes, I’m really looking forward to it and I’ve just started packing up all my books to put in storage.
sentence.content_zh: 简：是的，我真的很期待，我刚开始收拾我所有的书，准备放进仓库。

sentence.content: Kieran: Well, I hope they don’t get spoilt.
sentence.content_zh: 基兰：希望它们不会被弄坏。

sentence.content: Jane: It’s OK – my grandfather works in a bookshop and he told me how to pack them.
sentence.content_zh: 简：没事的，我爷爷在书店工作，他告诉了我如何打包。

sentence.content: Kieran: Oh, that’s helpful.
sentence.content_zh: 基兰：哦，真有帮助。

sentence.content: Jane: He says you have to support the spine otherwise the paper can come away from the cover.
sentence.content_zh: 简：他说必须把书脊支撑起来，否则纸张会从封面处脱落。

sentence.content: Kieran: Yeah – that’s obvious.
sentence.content_zh: 基兰：是的，很显然。

sentence.content: Jane: He also told me to pack them flat in the box not on their side – again because they can bend and if you leave them like that for, say, a year, it’s quite hard to get them back to their normal shape.
sentence.content_zh: 简：他还告诉我要把书平放在盒子里，不要侧放-因为这样它们会弯曲，如果你把它们这样放着，比如说放一年，就很难让它们恢复原来的形状了。

sentence.content: Kieran: Well, it’s pretty clear that ruins them, but a lot of people just can’t be bothered to protect their books.
sentence.content_zh: 基兰：嗯，很明显那会毁了那些书，但很多人就是懒得保护他们的书。

sentence.content: Jane: He always says it’s such a shame that publishers don’t use better-quality paper.
sentence.content_zh: 简：我爷爷总是说，出版商不使用质量更好的纸张真是太可惜了。

sentence.content: Kieran: It’s the acid in the paper that causes the problem, isn’t it?
sentence.content_zh: 基兰：是纸张中的酸造成了问题，不是吗？

sentence.content: Jane: Yeah – that’s why old books go yellow. You know some of the books my grandfather’s given me are like that already.
sentence.content_zh: 简：是的，这就是旧书变黄的原因。我祖父给我的一些书已经变成这样了。

sentence.content: Kieran: Oh . . .
Jane: I should dump them really if they’re going to deteriorate further, but I’d feel bad. They’ll always remind me of him. He’s quite a collector, you know.
sentence.content_zh: 简：如果那些书的状况进一步变坏的话，我真的应该把它们扔掉，但是我会觉得很可惜。那些书总会让我想起我爷爷。你知道，他是个收藏家。

sentence.content: Kieran: Well, if they’re important to you . . .
sentence.content_zh: 基兰：好吧，如果它们对你很重要……

sentence.content: Jane: Yeah – I’d regret just throwing them away.
sentence.content_zh: 简：是的-我会后悔把它们扔掉。

sentence.content: Kieran: You know, maybe it’s because I was taught to treasure books . . . but I hate seeing students force open the pages – of paperbacks. They press so hard they end up breaking the spine.
sentence.content_zh: 基兰：你知道吗，也许是因为我从小就被教育要珍惜书籍……，我很讨厌看到学生们用力打开平装书。他们用力过度，最后把书脊都压断了。

sentence.content: Jane: I know, but unfortunately, paperbacks aren’t designed to last a long time and people know that. Hardbacks aren’t quite as weak.
sentence.content_zh: 简：我知道，但不幸的是，平装书的设计不适合长期阅读，人们也知道这一点。精装书就没那么容易磨损。

sentence.content: Kieran: Yeah, they’re different, I suppose. But I still don’t think people value hardbacks like they used to.
sentence.content_zh: 基兰：是的，我想精装书确实不一样。但我还是觉得人们不像以前那样重视精装书了。

sentence.content: Jane: Well, they aren’t decorative, are they, like other objects. Plus, nowadays, people don’t keep them out on shelves as much as they used to.
sentence.content_zh: 简：嗯，精装书不像其他物品一样，是装饰品。另外，现在人们不再像以前那样把它们摆在书架上了。

sentence.content: Kieran: That’s such a pity. When I visit someone – if they have, say, a colourful book on a table, it’s the first thing I’m drawn to.
sentence.content_zh: 基兰：真遗憾。当我去拜访别人时，如果他们的桌子上摆放着一本色彩鲜艳的书，我首先就会被它吸引。

sentence.content: Jane: I agree – and book covers can be a work of art in themselves. Some are really eye-catching.
sentence.content_zh: 简：我同意，书的封面本身就是一件艺术品。有些封面非常引人注目。

sentence.content: Kieran: I’ve always been taught to handle books carefully. If you watch someone take a book off a shelf, well, they usually do it wrong.
sentence.content_zh: 基兰：我一直被教导要小心对待书籍。如果你看到有人从书架上取下一本书，你会发现他们拿书的方式通常是错的。

sentence.content: Jane: Ah, my grandfather says, you should put your hand right over the top of the book . . . or if you can’t do that, pull the other books on the shelf aside so that you can hold the whole cover.
sentence.content_zh: 简：啊，我爷爷说，你应该把手放在书的上面……如果你做不到，就把书架上的其他书拉到一边，这样你就可以握住整个封面了。

sentence.content: Kieran: When did you learn all this?
sentence.content_zh: 基兰：你什么时候学会这些的？

sentence.content: Jane: He watched me pull a heavy book off the shelf when I was small, and it fell on the floor and broke apart.
sentence.content_zh: 简：我小的时候，他看着我把一本很重的书从书架上拉下来，书掉在地上摔得四分五裂。

sentence.content: Kieran: Oh dear!
sentence.content_zh: 基兰：哦，天哪！

sentence.content: Jane: I can still remember it!
sentence.content_zh: 简：我还记得！

sentence.content: Kieran: You know what I really like?
sentence.content_zh: 基兰：你知道我最喜欢什么吗？

sentence.content: Jane: What?
sentence.content_zh: 简：什么？

sentence.content: Kieran: The smell of new books.
sentence.content_zh: 基兰：新书的味道。

sentence.content: Jane: Me too.
sentence.content_zh: 简：我也是。

sentence.content: Kieran: My parents used to laugh at me when I was a kid because I loved putting books up to my nose. Almost as much as reading them!
sentence.content_zh: 基兰：我小的时候，我父母经常嘲笑我，因为我喜欢把书放在鼻子上闻。几乎和看书一样！

sentence.content: Jane: New books aren’t cheap, though, are they?
sentence.content_zh: 简：新书可不便宜，不是吗？

sentence.content: Kieran: I guess we’re lucky we can buy them.
sentence.content_zh: 基兰：我想我们可以买书是很幸运的。

sentence.content: Jane: My grandfather stocks second-hand books as well as new ones and they don’t smell quite as good.
sentence.content_zh: 简：我爷爷除了买新书，还储备二手书，而且二手书闻起来没新书香。

sentence.content: Kieran: I’d love to have a bookshop like your grandfather. What’s it like?
sentence.content_zh: 基兰：我很想跟你祖父那样开一家书店。你祖父的书店是什么样的？

sentence.content: Jane: Well, it’s quite big – it’s got two floors and an attic, and he stocks all kinds of books really.
sentence.content_zh: 简：嗯，挺大的，有两层楼和一个阁楼，他有各种各样的书。

sentence.content: Kieran: I guess he treasures things like first editions and other rare books.
sentence.content_zh: 基兰：我猜他珍藏的是初版书和其他稀有书籍。

sentence.content: Jane: Yeah – you might think he’d keep those in the attic or somewhere.
sentence.content_zh: 简：是的，他也许会把这些书放在阁楼或其他地方。

sentence.content: Kieran: . . . so they’d be hidden?
sentence.content_zh: 基兰：所以它们会被藏起来？

sentence.content: Jane: Yeah. But he likes people to know that he has them. So, he puts them out in the shop but makes sure you need a ladder to get them.
sentence.content_zh: 简：是的。但他希望人们知道他有这些东西。所以，他把它们放在店里，但确保需要梯子才能拿到它们。

sentence.content: Kieran: Right. That would prevent any thefts!
sentence.content_zh: 基兰：好的。这样可以防止偷窃！

sentence.content: Jane: Uhuh.
sentence.content_zh: 简：嗯。

sentence.content: Kieran: Does he stock books for children?
sentence.content_zh: 基兰：他有儿童读物吗？

sentence.content: Jane: He does. He particularly likes to encourage kids to read; he always says that he used to sit under the stairs as a child with a pile of books and read them all.
sentence.content_zh: 简：他有。他特别喜欢鼓励孩子们阅读；他总是说，他小时候经常抱着一堆书坐在楼梯下，把它们都读完。

sentence.content: Kieran: Is that where he keeps them, then?
sentence.content_zh: 基兰：他把书都放在那里吗？

sentence.content: Jane: Not exactly – he’s got a dedicated area on the ground floor with cushions so that parents can enter with their toddlers, go there and spend some time reading to them.
sentence.content_zh: 简：不完全是-他在一楼专门开辟了一块地方，铺上了垫子，这样家长就可以带着他们的孩子去那里，花一些时间给他们读书。

sentence.content: Kieran: Oh cool.
sentence.content_zh: 基兰：哦，好酷。

sentence.content: Jane: And then there’s a place for pushchairs by the front door. And a café if anyone needs refreshments.
sentence.content_zh: 简：前门还有一个放婴儿车的地方。如果有人需要点心，还有一个咖啡厅。

sentence.content: Kieran: That’s good to know.
sentence.content_zh: 基兰：很高兴知道这些。

sentence.content: Jane: As I said, it’s a big shop and there’s a storage area out the back as well.
sentence.content_zh: 简：正如我所说，这是一家大商店，后面还有一个储藏区。

sentence.content: Kieran: Oh, what does he keep there? Books he wants to throw away?
sentence.content_zh: 基兰：哦，他在那儿放什么？他想扔掉的书？

sentence.content: Jane: He hardly ever throws anything away – he just leaves unwanted books by the front door for customers to take.
sentence.content_zh: 简：他几乎不扔任何东西-他只是把不想要的书放在前门让顾客拿走。

sentence.content: Kieran: Well, that’s very nice.
sentence.content_zh: 基兰：嗯，真不错。

sentence.content: Jane: Yeah – and books people or institutions have requested, they all go at the far end.
sentence.content_zh: 简：是的，人们或机构订购的书都放在最里面。

sentence.content: Kieran: Oh.
sentence.content_zh: 基兰：哦。

sentence.content: Jane: He thinks it’s best to keep these out of the main shopping area as they’re boxed and new.
sentence.content_zh: 简：他认为这些书最好不要放在主购物区，因为它们都是装箱的新书。

sentence.content: Kieran: Did you get your coursebooks from him?
sentence.content_zh: 基兰：你的课本是从他那儿拿的吗？

sentence.content: Jane: Naturally. He stocks books for a lot of the colleges. He used to keep these books on the first floor, but now there’s a new university in my hometown, he’s moved them downstairs to attract the students. They’re actually part of the coffee shop, on low shelves all around it.
sentence.content_zh: 简：当然。他储备了很多大学用的书籍。他以前把这些书放在一楼，但现在我的家乡新开了一所大学，为了吸引学生，他把书搬到了楼下。这些书实际上是咖啡店的一部分，放在咖啡店四周的矮架子上。

sentence.content: Kieran: Pretty central then. You’ll have to take me there some time!
sentence.content_zh: 基兰：那是很中心的位置了。有机会你一定要带我去！

sentence.content: Jane: Yeah. But he likes people to know that he has them. So, he puts them out in the shop but makes sure you need a ladder to get them.
sentence.content_zh: 简：是的。但他希望人们知道他有这些东西。所以，他把它们放在店里，但确保需要梯子才能拿到它们。

sentence.content: Kieran: Right. That would prevent any thefts!
sentence.content_zh: 基兰：好的。这样可以防止偷窃！

sentence.content: Jane: Uhuh.
sentence.content_zh: 简：嗯。

sentence.content: Kieran: Does he stock books for children?
sentence.content_zh: 基兰：他有儿童读物吗？

sentence.content: Jane: He does. He particularly likes to encourage kids to read; he always says that he used to sit under the stairs as a child with a pile of books and read them all.
sentence.content_zh: 简：他有。他特别喜欢鼓励孩子们阅读；他总是说，他小时候经常抱着一堆书坐在楼梯下，把它们都读完。

sentence.content: Kieran: Is that where he keeps them, then?
sentence.content_zh: 基兰：他把书都放在那里吗？

sentence.content: Jane: Not exactly – he’s got a dedicated area on the ground floor with cushions so that parents can enter with their toddlers, go there and spend some time reading to them.
sentence.content_zh: 简：不完全是-他在一楼专门开辟了一块地方，铺上了垫子，这样家长就可以带着他们的孩子去那里，花一些时间给他们读书。

sentence.content: Kieran: Oh cool.
sentence.content_zh: 基兰：哦，好酷。

sentence.content: Jane: And then there’s a place for pushchairs by the front door. And a café if anyone needs refreshments.
sentence.content_zh: 简：前门还有一个放婴儿车的地方。如果有人需要点心，还有一个咖啡厅。

sentence.content: Kieran: That’s good to know.
sentence.content_zh: 基兰：很高兴知道这些。

sentence.content: Jane: As I said, it’s a big shop and there’s a storage area out the back as well.
sentence.content_zh: 简：正如我所说，这是一家大商店，后面还有一个储藏区。

sentence.content: Kieran: Oh, what does he keep there? Books he wants to throw away?
sentence.content_zh: 基兰：哦，他在那儿放什么？他想扔掉的书？

sentence.content: Jane: He hardly ever throws anything away – he just leaves unwanted books by the front door for customers to take.
sentence.content_zh: 简：他几乎不扔任何东西-他只是把不想要的书放在前门让顾客拿走。

sentence.content: Kieran: Well, that’s very nice.
sentence.content_zh: 基兰：嗯，真不错。

sentence.content: Jane: Yeah – and books people or institutions have requested, they all go at the far end.
sentence.content_zh: 简：是的，人们或机构订购的书都放在最里面。

sentence.content: Kieran: Oh.
sentence.content_zh: 基兰：哦。

sentence.content: Jane: He thinks it’s best to keep these out of the main shopping area as they’re boxed and new.
sentence.content_zh: 简：他认为这些书最好不要放在主购物区，因为它们都是装箱的新书。

sentence.content: Kieran: Did you get your coursebooks from him?
sentence.content_zh: 基兰：你的课本是从他那儿拿的吗？

sentence.content: Jane: Naturally. He stocks books for a lot of the colleges. He used to keep these books on the first floor, but now there’s a new university in my hometown, he’s moved them downstairs to attract the students. They’re actually part of the coffee shop, on low shelves all around it.
sentence.content_zh: 简：当然。他储备了很多大学用的书籍。他以前把这些书放在一楼，但现在我的家乡新开了一所大学，为了吸引学生，他把书搬到了楼下。这些书实际上是咖啡店的一部分，放在咖啡店四周的矮架子上。

sentence.content: Kieran: Pretty central then. You’ll have to take me there some time!
sentence.content_zh: 基兰：那是很中心的位置了。有机会你一定要带我去！

officialExplain.explain: 本题需要注意题干提到两位Kieran和Jane’s grandfather，重点关注Kieran的看法。原文中packing和pack都对应题干中的packing，Jane表述中的my grandfather对应题干中的Jane’s grandfather。结合题干大致定位后，Jane转述了祖父的一些建议，即support the spine otherwise the paper can come away from the cover，对此Kieran的回应是obvious，意为“明显的，显然的”或“当然的，公认的”。Jane转述的另一些建议是pack them flat in the box not on their side，对此Kieran的回应是pretty clear，clear意为“明显的，清楚的”。这些回应对应A选项中的common sense，意为“常识”，因此本题答案为A。

本题需要注意题干中问的是Jane的感受如何。原文中Jane提及旧书的纸张变黄，并说到some of the books my grandfather’s given me（这里原文中的’s指has），对应本题题干中的the books her grandfather has given her。关于这些书，Jane进一步的表述中虽然先讲到dump them，但相关内容是如果（前面提及的）书本情况变差，并不代表这些书籍不值得保存，与A选项不符。原文中but之后讲到的感受是feel bad，并表示这些书籍remind me of him，remind sb. of sb./sth.的意思是“使某人想起……”，这些内容可以对应C选项中的sentimental value，sentimental意为“情感的”，sentimental value可以理解为“情感价值”。之后B选项中的collector虽然原词重现，但相关内容是He’s quite a collector，quite a用于强调在某方面很突出，这里Jane说的收藏家指的是她的祖父，而不是B选项中的go to a collector，排除B选项。因此本题答案为C。

本题需要注意题干中说的是两人同意的内容。原文中的hardbacks对应题干中的hardback books，都表示“精装书”。Kieran表示他觉得人们不像以前那样珍视精装书，但Jane提及精装书aren’t decorative，此处没有表现出意见一致。之后Jane继续讲到现在人们不再像以前那样，把书放在书架上；Kieran先表示such a pity，紧接着说到colourful book on a table和first thing I’m drawn to，draw在此处意为“吸引，使感兴趣”；Jane回应I agree，这里明确地表示了同意。这部分对话中，两人的表述都对应A选项，并且看法达成了一致。之后的内容中还提及了书本封面本身就可以成为艺术品，还提及eye-catching，意为“引人注目的”，但并未体现出C选项more包含的比较含义。因此本题答案为A。

本题需要特别注意Jane所叙述的内容。原文中take a book off a shelf对应本题题干中的taking a book from a shelf，接着出现了they usually do it wrong这样的表述，看似和A选项中的mistake对应，但这里是Kieran叙述的内容，不符合本题题干，排除A选项。之后的原文中，Jane回应了在什么时候了解到如何把书从书架小心拿出的问题，还提到她小时候从书架拿一本厚书的经历，when I was small对应B选项中的childhood，关于这一经历的表述同时对应B选项，即本题答案。

本题题干问题中的new books在原文中原词重现。Kieran提及parents，A选项部分关键词原词重现，但原文相关内容为used to laugh at me，与A选项中的buying them as presents不符，排除A选项。接下来Jane讲到New books aren’t cheap，Kieran给出的回应中提及lucky we can buy them，都对应C选项。这里虽然也提及B选项中的buy，但B选项中的more of them在原文中没有明确的对应信息。因此本题答案为C。

本题题干rare books在原文中原词重现。接下来B选项中的in the attic原词重现，attic意思是“阁楼，顶楼”，但同一句中所述信息是“you might think …”。之后Jane讲到puts them out in the shop，C选项虽然有shop但back这一信息原文中没有出现。因此B和C两项都排除。Jane在but之后说need a ladder to get them，ladder意为“梯子”，这里对应D选项中的on a high shelf，因此本题答案为D。

原文中books for children对应本题题干children’s books。之后的原文中，Jane先提及stairs，但相关信息为sit under the stairs，且之后Kieran问这是不是放书的地方，Jane给出了否定回应Not exactly，结合这些信息可以排除E选项near the stairs。接下来Jane提到放书地点为dedicated area on the ground floor with cushions，dedicated意为“专用的，专门用途的”。结合dedicated area以及with cushions，可以对应F选项中的specially designed space，因此本题答案为F。
在27题内容结束后，原文中Jane先提及了by the front door和café。当Kieran明确问到放什么书在那里后，在Jane的表述中unwanted books原词重现，对应的位置是by the front door，front door意为“正门，前门”，这部分信息对应A选项中的near the entrance，因此本题答案为A。
原文中“books … requested”对应题干中的requested books，接下来Jane提到的位置是at the far end，far end可以理解为“远端，另一头”，结合之后讲到的out of the main shopping area，对应C选项内容，因此本题答案为C。
本题题干coursebooks在原文中原词重现。接下来原文中先讲到以前放置在first floor，现在移到了楼下。之后原文中提及part of the coffee shop，对应G选项中的within the café，因此本题答案为G。
